description: Ship the installable, three-mode (Create + Edit + Study) AI-assisted note-taking PWA MVP for nursing students.

stories.title: Add Zod-validated env loader and secrets boundary
stories.description: Lazily Zod-validate all environment variables (Supabase URL/keys, OpenAI key, ALLOWED_ORIGIN) so secrets live only in env and next build stays green. Foundation read by every later auth, persistence, and AI story (NFR-03, tech S5/S10).
stories.goal: Provide a single Zod-validated, lazily-parsed env accessor so secrets stay in env only and next build succeeds without them.
stories.notes: Parse inside accessors (not at module top level) so `next build` stays green without secrets; vars per .env.example: NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY, SUPABASE_SECRET_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, ALLOWED_ORIGIN.
tasks: Define a Zod 4 schema covering the public and secret env vars (Supabase URL/publishable key, SUPABASE_SECRET_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, ALLOWED_ORIGIN)
Expose typed accessors in src/lib/env.ts that parse lazily inside the getter so a missing secret never breaks build-time imports
Add an .env.example documenting every required variable
Throw a clear, named error when a required var is missing at runtime access
acceptance_criteria: `npm run build` succeeds with no secret env vars set
Accessing a server-only secret accessor without the var set throws a descriptive error naming the variable
A check (grepping the built client chunks or a lint rule) confirms SUPABASE_SECRET_KEY and OPENAI_API_KEY never appear in client-bundled JS; only NEXT_PUBLIC_* vars reach the client

stories.title: Provision Supabase project and wire SDK clients
stories.description: Stand up the single Supabase backend and the browser/server supabase-js clients. Prerequisite for auth, RLS, persistence, and AI orchestration (tech S1/S3).
stories.goal: Establish the Supabase backend and the browser + server supabase-js clients that every auth, data, and AI feature depends on.
stories.notes: Server client must use cookie-based sessions for SSR/middleware; clients read connection config through the validated env loader.
tasks: Create the Supabase project and capture its URL and keys into env
Build the browser supabase-js client in src/lib/supabase/client.ts
Build the cookie-based server supabase-js client in src/lib/supabase/server.ts
Add a smoke check (e.g. the /api/health route) that confirms the client can reach Supabase
acceptance_criteria: The browser client initializes from validated env without runtime errors
The server client reads and writes the session cookie on a server request
A health check confirms connectivity to the provisioned Supabase project

stories.title: Establish design-system tokens and light/dark theming
stories.description: Define the full light/dark token sets, Inter type scale, spacing/radius/elevation, Lucide icon conventions, and the single constant brand accent #3ba9ff. Shared visual foundation every screen renders on; runtime theme switching behavior is owned separately (design S1/S7/S8, tech S2).
stories.goal: Codify the complete light/dark CSS-variable token system and type/spacing scales so every screen renders on one consistent visual foundation.
stories.notes: Brand accent #3ba9ff is constant across light, dark, and both modes — tokens must NOT introduce any per-mode accent (FR-01/design invariant). Includes the three brand tiers, four semantic colors, the highlight token, and both elevation scales per design S1. Focus-ring oddity (#ebebeb light, dark inherits) is intentional per spec — do not silently change it. Sized at the upper edge of M for the full two-palette + full type-scale scope.
tasks: Define light and dark CSS-variable palettes: background, surface, muted, foreground, muted-foreground, border, focus ring (#ebebeb light / dark inherits) per design S1

Define the secondary (#2a8fd9, also info), tertiary (#1e1e1e) brand tiers, the highlight token (#fef3c7), and the four semantic colors (success #10b981, warning #f59e0b, error #ef4444, info #2a8fd9) by hex
Define both elevation scales (shadow rgba(0,0,0,0.1)/0.5; shadow-large rgba(0,0,0,0.15)/0.7) with deeper dark-mode opacity
Set the Inter editor + UI type scales, the 4px spacing base, the three radii (6/8/12px plus full), and register the constant brand accent #3ba9ff via CSS variables in Tailwind 4
Establish Lucide icon sizing conventions (12/14/16/20/24px) as shared usage rules
acceptance_criteria: Light and dark token sets are defined as CSS variables and switch the entire palette via a single root class/attribute
The brand accent resolves to #3ba9ff identically in light and dark and in both Write and Study contexts
The spacing base unit is 4px and the three radii (6/8/12px) are defined
Both elevation scales (shadow, shadow-large) exist with deeper dark-mode opacity
All four semantic colors, the highlight token, and the secondary/tertiary brand tiers resolve to their design.md S1 hex values in both themes

stories.title: Build shared UI design-system primitives
stories.description: Build the reusable Radix-based primitives (button, input, popover, dialog, tooltip, badge, segmented control, key chips) styled to the tokens. Consumed by the sidebar, library, settings, editor header, and Study panel, so they precede those screens (design S1/S2/S5, tech S2).
stories.goal: Deliver a token-styled Radix primitive set (including a menu/dropdown and badge-counter) that every screen composes, so UI work stays consistent and accessible.
stories.notes: Tooltip uses the shared 200ms delay; primitives expose accessible labels and the theme focus ring per design S5. Tag/favorite tints exist only as static styling, never a persistence-backed subsystem. Includes the menu/dropdown primitive consumed by the note-card overflow, sort selector, and settings rows, and the destructive (#ef4444) variant.
tasks: Build button (32px square / 6px radius, idle muted / hover surface+foreground / active brand-tinted background + brand icon), input, and badge primitives on the tokens

Build Radix-based popover, dialog, tooltip (200ms delay), and a menu/dropdown wrapper (used by note-card overflow, sort selector, settings rows) including a destructive #ef4444 item variant, all with accessible labels and focus ring
Build the segmented control (32px) and the 10px mono key-chip primitives, and a full-radius badge-counter/tag-pill primitive
Add a primitives showcase or stories to verify states in light and dark
acceptance_criteria: The named primitives (button, input, badge, popover, dialog, tooltip, menu/dropdown with destructive variant, segmented control, key chip, badge-counter) exist and render in both light and dark using only design tokens (no hardcoded hex)
Button is 32px square / 6px radius and reflects idle, hover, and active (brand-tinted) states; the tooltip opens after 200ms; the segmented control is 32px and key chips are 10px mono
Interactive primitives expose accessible labels and a visible theme focus ring on keyboard focus

stories.title: Wire Vitest, RTL, and Playwright test tooling
stories.description: Add the test harness, configs (Vitest jsdom + RTL, Playwright Chromium), and npm scripts so unit/E2E tests and coverage can run. Foundation for every test-group story and the CI test gate (tech S9, CLAUDE.md).
stories.goal: Stand up the unit and E2E test runners and scripts so every later test story and the CI test gate have a working harness.
stories.notes: Vitest jsdom + React Testing Library for unit; Playwright Chromium for E2E (e2e:install fetches Chromium). In this sandbox npm needs --cache "$TMPDIR/npm-cache".
tasks: Configure Vitest with jsdom and React Testing Library and add test / test:watch / test:coverage scripts
Configure Playwright with the Chromium project and add e2e / e2e:install scripts
Add v8 coverage configuration with critical-path thresholds
Add a trivial passing unit test and a trivial E2E test to prove both runners execute
acceptance_criteria: `npm test` runs the Vitest suite under jsdom and the sample unit test passes
`npm run e2e` runs the Playwright suite against Chromium and the sample E2E test passes
Coverage output is produced via `npm run test:coverage`

stories.title: Add test and coverage stages to the CI pipeline
stories.description: Extend the existing lint to typecheck to build CI gate (which currently has no test step) with automated test runs on PR and a critical-path coverage check. Depends on the test tooling existing first (tech S9/S10, NFR-02, SC-04).
stories.goal: Make automated tests and critical-path coverage a required CI gate on every PR so reliability regressions are caught before merge.
stories.notes: Current CI is npm ci -> lint -> typecheck -> build on Node 22; this inserts the test/coverage stage. Targets 80%+ coverage on critical paths (tech S9).
tasks: Add a test stage to .github/workflows/ci.yml after typecheck and before/with build
Run the unit suite with coverage in CI and fail the job below the critical-path threshold
Ensure the Playwright E2E run (or its install step) is wired into CI as appropriate for the gate
acceptance_criteria: A PR with a failing unit test blocks merge via the CI gate
CI fails when critical-path coverage drops below the configured threshold
The full pipeline npm ci -> lint -> typecheck -> test -> build runs green on a passing PR

stories.title: Scaffold App Router route structure and app shell
stories.description: Set up the App Router route groups (auth vs authenticated shell), root layout, and the two-column app shell hosting the sidebar plus routed main column. Structural backbone every screen mounts inside (tech S2, design S2/S3, CLAUDE.md layout).
stories.goal: Lay down the (auth)/(app) route groups, root layout, and two-column shell so every discovery and editor screen has a place to mount.
stories.notes: Routes per CLAUDE.md: (auth)/login|signup|auth/callback; (app)/library|notes/[noteId]|notes/new. Desktop-first only — no mobile layouts. Per design 'Onboarding' (FR-08/NFR-05) onboarding is overlay-only contextual tooltips, NOT a screen; the scaffold's (app)/onboarding route is intentionally omitted/no-op here so we do not resurrect a blocking onboarding page (see open question).
tasks: Create the (auth) and (app) App Router route groups with their layouts
Build the root layout applying the design tokens and theme root
Compose the two-column app shell (fixed sidebar slot + routed main column) under (app)
Stub the library, notes/[noteId], and notes/new routes (no standalone onboarding route — onboarding is overlay tooltips per FR-08)
acceptance_criteria: Navigating to an (app) route renders the two-column shell with sidebar slot and routed main column
The (auth) routes render outside the app shell
The library, notes/[noteId], and notes/new routes resolve without 404 and the build compiles the route tree; no standalone onboarding page route exists

stories.title: Configure Vercel build and staging previews
stories.description: Configure next build deployed to Vercel with edge cache and PR staging previews. Placed after the app shell (NEXLY-007) exists so there is a real app to preview, and builds on the green CI gate (tech S10, NFR-06).
stories.goal: Enable next build on Vercel with edge caching and per-PR staging previews so changes can be previewed and the path to prod is established.
stories.notes: Builds on the green CI gate; production secrets/migrations are applied later by the production deploy story. The deferred build-time env config must be wired so the build compiles (the env-loader exists precisely so build stays green).
tasks: Connect the repo to Vercel and configure the next build production build
Configure the build-time and public env vars (NEXT_PUBLIC_*) on Vercel referencing the env-loader contract so the preview/prod build compiles
Enable edge caching and verify the app shell loads on the preview URL with caching enabled
Enable per-PR staging preview deployments
acceptance_criteria: Opening a PR produces a working staging preview URL where the app shell loads
A build succeeds on the preview/branch with the configured env vars
Edge caching is enabled and a redeploy serves updated assets on the preview URL

stories.title: Add installable PWA manifest and app icons
stories.description: Add the web app manifest and icon set so the app is installable. Baseline of the installable-shell requirement and the harder installability prerequisite ahead of the service worker (NFR-06, tech S1/S2/S11).
stories.goal: Make NEXLY installable by adding a valid web app manifest and the brand icon set.
stories.notes: Logo is a brand-blue square with letter N (design S2). Desktop/web install target; online-only. The live install-affordance check is deferred to the 'Verify installable-PWA and manifest validity' story (which sequences after the service worker).
tasks: Author the web app manifest (name, short_name, theme/background color, display standalone, start_url)
Generate the icon set at the required install sizes from the brand logo
Link the manifest and icons from the root layout head
acceptance_criteria: The manifest passes automated manifest/installability validation (required fields, valid icon set, display=standalone, start_url) and is served at a stable path referenced from the document head
All required icon sizes are present and resolve
The manifest theme/background colors resolve from the design tokens

stories.title: Implement online-only static-shell service worker with freshness strategy
stories.description: Add a service worker that caches only the app shell for installability while staying online-only (no offline editing/sync), including a versioning/cache-bust strategy so a redeploy never serves a stale shell (NFR-06, tech S2/S11).
stories.goal: Cache only the static app shell for installability while staying strictly online-only, with cache versioning so a redeploy never serves a stale shell.
stories.notes: Strictly NO offline note caching, background sync, or offline editing path (post-beta). Shell-only caching with a cache-bust/versioning strategy on deploy. Must reconcile the SW shell cache against the Vercel edge cache so a redeploy invalidates both layers (tech S10 ships SW shell cache + edge cache together).
tasks: Enumerate the precise static-shell asset set (shell HTML + hashed build assets) and precache only those in the service worker
Use a network-first/online-only strategy for note data and dynamic routes (no offline data caching)
Add a cache version key and an activation step that purges stale shell caches on update, and verify the SW version bump composes correctly with Vercel edge caching (redeploy invalidates both layers)
Ensure note reads/writes always require the network (no offline write queue)
acceptance_criteria: The service worker registers and precaches only the enumerated static shell assets
With the network offline, note data does not load (online-only behavior is preserved)
After a redeploy with a bumped cache version, clients receive the new shell rather than a stale cached one, with no stale edge-cached asset defeating the bump

stories.title: Create Postgres schema migration (users, notes, nursing_terms)
stories.description: Define the only three MVP tables — notes (content jsonb + word_count, no note_mode), nursing_terms (term + definition), users — plus the auth-to-users linkage so per-user isolation is part of the data layer. No note_versions/usage_quotas (tech S3/S5, FR-03, FR-07).
stories.goal: Create the three MVP tables with correct types/defaults and the auth-to-users linkage as the canonical data model the whole product builds on.
stories.notes: notes(id uuid, user_id uuid, title text, content jsonb, word_count int, created_at timestamptz, updated_at timestamptz) — NO note_mode column (mode is a runtime lens). NO note_versions or usage_quotas tables, NO is_favorite, NO tags (Phase 2 / out of scope). The FR-07 last-edited sort and auto-save both depend on updated_at being auto-maintained.
tasks: Enable uuid/pgcrypto and default id to gen_random_uuid(); type columns per tech S5 (content jsonb NOT NULL, word_count int default 0, created_at/updated_at timestamptz default now())
Write the migration creating users, notes (no note_mode), and nursing_terms (id uuid, term text, definition text)
Add an updated_at auto-update trigger on notes so FR-07 last-edited sort and auto-save updated_at are correct
Establish the auth.users -> users linkage and the notes.user_id foreign key; add indexes supporting library sort (updated_at, created_at, title) and nursing_terms term lookup
Confirm note_versions and usage_quotas are not created in this migration
acceptance_criteria: Applying the migration creates exactly users, notes, and nursing_terms with the tech S5 column types and defaults (uuid id default, jsonb content, int word_count, timestamptz timestamps)
notes has no note_mode column and no version/quota/favorite/tag columns; updating a note bumps updated_at via the trigger
notes.user_id references the user identity and the foreign key is enforced

stories.title: Author Row-Level Security policies for per-user isolation
stories.description: Author RLS policies keying off the authenticated user_id, placed in the data layer ahead of any note read/write feature because the security model is a hard dependency that must precede data features. End-to-end isolation is exercisable only once auth and the users-row linkage land (NEXLY-051/053) — stated here to flag the deferred verification (tech S3/S10, NFR-03).
stories.goal: Lock every user/note row behind RLS keyed on auth.uid() so each student can only ever read or write their own data.
stories.notes: Security-before-data ordering: policies are authored here, but the true cross-user END-TO-END isolation assertion belongs to the auth/users-row provisioning story and the E2E suite. nursing_terms is shared read-only reference data, not per-user. Sized S to match the bounded scope (enable RLS + 4 policies per table) consistent with the other auth/isolation stories.
tasks: Enable RLS on users and notes
Add select/insert/update/delete policies scoping users and notes to auth.uid()
Set nursing_terms as read-only reference data accessible to authenticated users
Add a JWT-claim-simulated query/test proving a scoped read returns only the matching rows and anon is rejected
acceptance_criteria: RLS is enabled on users and notes with four policies per table keyed on auth.uid()
An unauthenticated (anon) request to users or notes is rejected
A query run with a simulated JWT user claim returns only that user's rows (full cross-user end-to-end isolation is verified in the auth/users-row and E2E stories)

stories.title: Seed nursing_terms with 5,000+ terms and definitions
stories.description: Seed the nursing_terms table with 5,000+ terms plus short definition snippets — the shared data substrate powering the primary local autocomplete path and the Study hover dictionary (tech S3/S4/S5, FR-02, FR-06).
stories.goal: Populate nursing_terms with 5,000+ terms and short definitions so both local autocomplete and Study hover have real content.
stories.notes: Single shared data asset for FR-02 (local autocomplete primary path) and FR-06 (hover tooltip) — product-bearing data, hence the feature work-type. Definitions are short snippets sized for the tooltip. English-only for MVP. Sourcing 5,000 quality definitions is the real cost/risk; open question flags whether this story owns curation vs loading a provided dataset.
tasks: Identify a licensed/permissible term+definition source and record its provenance
Assemble 5,000+ nursing terms each with a short definition snippet capped to the tooltip length, normalizing term casing/whitespace at seed time so the grounding lookup matches reliably
Write supabase/seed.sql (or a seed migration) inserting the terms into nursing_terms
Validate counts and dedupe terms before insert
acceptance_criteria: After seeding, nursing_terms contains at least 5,000 rows
Every seeded row has a non-empty term and a non-empty short definition bounded to the tooltip snippet length
Seeded terms are unique (no duplicate term entries) and term casing/whitespace is normalized

stories.title: Define domain/DB types and Zod schemas for notes and terms
stories.description: Define the shared TS types and Zod schemas for note content (Tiptap jsonb), word_count, title, and term shapes. The validation contract reused by persistence APIs and forms (tech S2/S5, NFR-03).
stories.goal: Provide shared TS types and Zod schemas for notes and terms so persistence and forms validate against one source of truth.
stories.notes: Lives in src/types (note.ts, database.ts) + Zod schemas. Note content is a Tiptap jsonb document; no note_mode/tags/favorite fields.
tasks: Define TS domain types for Note (id, user_id, title, content jsonb, word_count, timestamps) and NursingTerm
Define Zod schemas for note create/update payloads and term shapes
Generate or align the DB types (database.ts) with the migration
Export the schemas for reuse by persistence APIs and forms
acceptance_criteria: Zod schemas validate a well-formed note payload and reject one missing title or content
The Note type matches the notes table columns exactly (no note_mode/tags/favorite)
The schemas are importable from a single shared module used by both API and forms

stories.title: Define the note persistence API contract
stories.description: Specify the load/list/save/create/delete note interface (latest-content overwrite, no snapshots) that auto-save, the library, Quick Create, and note deletion all call (tech S5/S7, FR-03, FR-07).
stories.goal: Lock the load/list/save/create/delete note interface (latest-content overwrite, no snapshots) so producers and consumers build against one stable contract.
stories.notes: Save overwrites latest notes.content + word_count only — explicitly no snapshot/version history. List supports the FR-07 title-search + sort parameters. The contract documents where word_count is computed (server-derived from the Tiptap content). Sized S/3 to align with the sibling type-schema and key-term-schema contract stories.
tasks: Specify function signatures for getNote, listNotes (search/sort params), saveNote (overwrite content + word_count), createNote (blank), deleteNote
Define request/response shapes referencing the shared Zod schemas, and specify that word_count is server-derived from the Tiptap content (not trusted from the client)
Document the latest-only overwrite semantics and the recovery = last successful save guarantee
Confirm no snapshot/version parameters exist in any signature
acceptance_criteria: The contract documents getNote, listNotes, saveNote, createNote, and deleteNote with typed inputs/outputs
saveNote semantics state latest-content overwrite with no version snapshot and a server-derived word_count
listNotes exposes title-search and the four sort orders as parameters

stories.title: Define Edge Function CORS and runaway-backstop contract
stories.description: Define the shared Deno CORS preflight handling and the per-user daily-ceiling circuit-breaker contract (cost insurance, not a quota; threshold value deferred) all AI Edge Functions must conform to (tech S3/S10, FR-09, NFR-04).
stories.goal: Define one shared CORS preflight contract and a per-user daily-ceiling circuit-breaker contract that both AI Edge Functions must conform to.
stories.notes: The backstop is COST INSURANCE, not a product quota — no token tracking, no tiers, unlimited for beta. CORS restricted to ALLOWED_ORIGIN. The numeric ceiling value is deferred (see open question), but the keying/window is specified now.
tasks: Specify the CORS preflight handler contract (allowed origin from env, methods, headers)
Define the per-user daily-ceiling circuit-breaker contract: keyed per auth user_id, calendar-day window, request-count based (NOT token/quota based), with the numeric ceiling left TBD
Document the response shape when the backstop trips (clearly distinct from a product-quota error)
State explicitly that this is not a quota and carries no billing/tier semantics
acceptance_criteria: The contract specifies a single CORS preflight behavior keyed on ALLOWED_ORIGIN
The backstop is defined precisely as a per-user (user_id), calendar-day, request-count ceiling with no token counting or tier logic (numeric value may be TBD)
The contract documents the trip response distinctly from any quota/billing concept

stories.title: Define the structured key-term output schema and AI request contract
stories.description: Define the GPT-4o-mini Structured-Outputs typed term schema (term, definition source, emphasis cue, document anchor) and the shared context-capping (~current paragraph / ~500 tokens) request shape. The interface the spotting function, Web Worker, Study panel, and annotations all build against. FR-05's 'with context' is read here as the document anchor powering click-to-jump, not a separately rendered surrounding-text snippet; do not add a snippet field or panel UI until product confirms the intent (FR-05, tech S4/S5, NFR-04, design S2).
stories.goal: Lock the typed key-term Structured-Outputs schema and TWO distinct request shapes (paragraph-capped ghost-text vs full-note chunked spotting) so the functions, Worker, panel, and annotations all build against one interface.
stories.notes: Schema fields: term, definition source, emphasis cue, document anchor (for click-to-jump). FR-05 'with context' is read as the anchor, not a rendered surrounding-text snippet — do NOT add a snippet field or panel UI until product confirms (open question). CRITICAL (tech S4 line 92): the ~current paragraph / ~500-token cap is the GHOST-TEXT fidelity-guard budget ONLY; key-term spotting targets a full 3,000-word note (tech S4 line 82) and must NOT be 500-token capped — its request shape carries the full note, chunked.
tasks: Define the Zod / JSON-schema typed term shape (term, definition source, emphasis cue, document anchor)
Define the GHOST-TEXT request shape with the ~current paragraph / ~500-token fidelity-guard cap
Define the SPOTTING request shape separately, carrying the full note (chunked for the model), explicitly NOT subject to the ~500-token ghost-text cap
Document the click-to-jump anchor semantics and the exam-relevant emphasis-cue field, and publish the schema as the shared contract for the function, Worker, panel, and annotations
acceptance_criteria: The schema defines term, definition source, emphasis cue, and a document anchor field
No surrounding-text snippet field is added to the schema or panel UI in this contract
Two request shapes are documented and distinct: ghost-text carries the ~500-token/current-paragraph cap, spotting carries the full (chunked) 3,000-word note with no 500-token cap

stories.title: Implement note persistence backend (RLS + Zod CRUD + delete)
stories.description: Implement the server-side note read/list/save/create/delete via supabase-js enforcing RLS and Zod-validated inputs — the persistence backbone behind auto-save, the library, Quick Create, and card deletion (tech S5/S10, FR-03, FR-07, NFR-03).
stories.goal: Implement the RLS-enforced, Zod-validated note CRUD backend that every save, list, create, and delete flow calls, with server-derived word_count.
stories.notes: All access via supabase-js (parameterized) under RLS; inputs validated by the shared Zod schemas. saveNote overwrites latest content + word_count only (no snapshots). word_count is derived server-side from the Tiptap content so it cannot be spoofed by the client (NFR-03). This story owns the API/DB validation boundary; the auth/note FORM validation story owns the client+auth boundary (see open question).
tasks: Implement getNote and listNotes (with title-search + sort) scoped by RLS
Implement saveNote overwriting content + a server-derived word_count, and createNote for a blank note
Implement deleteNote
Validate all inputs with the shared Zod schemas before any DB call
acceptance_criteria: CRUD operations return only the authenticated user's rows under RLS
An invalid note payload is rejected by Zod validation before reaching the database, and word_count is computed server-side from the content (not taken from the client)

saveNote overwrites the latest content/word_count and creates no version row; deleteNote removes only the owner's note and a delete targeting another user's note or a missing id affects zero rows under RLS

stories.title: Implement shared Edge Function scaffold (CORS, runaway backstop, privacy)
stories.description: Build the shared Deno CORS module, per-user daily-ceiling circuit breaker, and the OpenAI client configured for notes-never-train and HTTPS-only transport so the privacy posture is baked in before any function calls OpenAI (tech S3/S4/S10, FR-09, NFR-03/04).
stories.goal: Provide the shared Deno harness (CORS, per-user backstop, privacy-safe OpenAI client) that both AI functions build on so privacy and cost invariants are enforced uniformly.
stories.notes: _shared/cors.ts preflight + the per-user request-count circuit breaker + an OpenAI client that logs/persists no note content and uses HTTPS only. No token/quota tracking. OpenAI API data is not used for training by default; document any org-level zero-retention setting rather than asserting a non-existent per-client opt-out flag.
tasks: Implement the shared CORS preflight handler keyed on ALLOWED_ORIGIN
Implement the per-user daily-ceiling circuit breaker per the backstop contract
Configure the shared OpenAI client to use HTTPS only, read the key from validated env, and log/persist no note content; document the org-level data-retention/no-training posture
Expose the scaffold as a reusable module both AI functions import
acceptance_criteria: A cross-origin preflight from a disallowed origin is rejected; the allowed origin passes
Exceeding the per-user daily request ceiling trips the backstop with the contract's trip response
The OpenAI client base URL is HTTPS and the function logs/persists no note content; the data-retention/no-training posture is documented

stories.title: Capture privacy-safe beta success events and reporting
stories.description: Instrument the events that prove SC-01..04 over the 2-week, 50-user beta: mode-toggle usage (SC-01), autocomplete shown vs accepted counts (SC-02), Study key-term spotting activation (SC-03), and auto-save success/failure plus critical error/crash events (SC-04), with a lightweight in-app self-report prompt for the SC-02 'keep up / faster' metric. Privacy constraint (NFR-03): payloads carry only event name, ISO timestamp, anonymous user/session id, numeric counts, latency/error metadata, and feature-state flags — never note content, title, or PII. Add a reporting query/export so the release gate is measurable without ad-hoc DB access. This is release-gate instrumentation that makes SC-01..04 measurable, not analytics polish (prd S6, SC-01/02/03/04, NFR-03).
stories.goal: Instrument the SC-01..04 success events with a privacy-safe payload and a reporting query so the beta release gate is measurable.
stories.notes: Privacy invariant (NFR-03): payloads carry only event name, ISO timestamp, anonymous user/session id, numeric counts, latency/error/feature-state metadata — NEVER note content, title, or PII. SC-02's autocomplete acceptance metric is RESOLVED by the PRD (SC-04 table: accepted ÷ shown) — instrument shown and accepted counts accordingly. This story introduces an events sink table BEYOND the tech S5 three-table model; that expansion is justified by prd S6 / SC measurability and is flagged in open questions for documentation.
tasks: Define the event taxonomy: mode-toggle usage (SC-01), autocomplete shown/accepted counts for the accepted-divided-by-shown metric (SC-02), spotting activation (SC-03), auto-save success/failure + crash (SC-04)

stories.title: Implement Edge Function prompt caching for AI requests
stories.description: Establish the shared cached-prefix prompt structure and prompt caching across both AI Edge Functions — a named NFR-04 cost-control pillar that must not be left implicit in one function, critical for unlimited beta AI (NFR-04, tech S3/S4, prd S7).
stories.goal: Structure both AI functions' prompts around a cached stable prefix so prompt caching reduces cost during the unlimited-AI beta.
stories.notes: One of NFR-04's three named cost levers (context cap + prompt caching + runaway backstop). Applies to both ghost-text and spotting functions on the shared scaffold. OpenAI prompt caching is provider-managed and only engages above a token threshold within a TTL — so verification asserts a stable, cache-eligible prefix and inspects usage metadata on a warmed repeat rather than guaranteeing a first-repeat hit.
tasks: Define a stable cacheable prompt prefix shared across AI requests, large enough to be cache-eligible
Apply prompt caching configuration in the shared OpenAI client/scaffold
Ensure variable per-request context is appended after the cached prefix
Verify the prefix structure for both the ghost-text and spotting request paths and inspect usage metadata for cached-token counts on a warmed repeat
acceptance_criteria: Both AI functions issue requests with a stable, cache-eligible prefix and a variable suffix
Prompt caching is enabled in the shared client configuration
On a warmed repeat, the response usage metadata exposes cached-token counts (or prompt-structure + log inspection confirms the prefix is stable and cache-eligible)

The spotting dedupe key is note id + content hash.

stories.description: Implement server-side AI phrase ghost-text via GPT-4.1 nano on the shared scaffold (CORS, backstop, privacy, caching), with context capped per the shared request contract and a fidelity guard so completions stay assistive and never drift into generic textbook text (FR-02, tech S4, NFR-01/03/04, prd S7).

stories.notes: Fidelity guard (prd S7 line 76): cap context to the student's current paragraph (~500 tokens) and suppress completions that diverge from the student's content into generic textbook text. Assistive only — never authors full notes for the student. Uses the GHOST-TEXT request shape (paragraph cap), NOT the spotting full-note shape. Sized M/5; if the fidelity guard proves to be a simple threshold it trends toward S.
